hero.title: Moving company to and from Germany – Berlin, Hamburg, Munich and beyond
hero.subtitle: We help Swedish families and businesses move to Germany and German customers move to Sweden. Our experience with Autobahn logistics, environmental zones in German cities and differences in property structures means we can plan moves that actually work on the ground – from Berlin Altbau to new-build apartments in Munich.

features.title: What benefits do you get with Flyttella for moves to/from Germany?

about.title: About Flyttella for moves to and from Germany
about.desktop: Flyttella has been operating for over 8 years and helped 8,000 customers with moves in the Stockholm area and internationally. For moves to and from Germany we have developed a clear specialization around German conditions: environmental zones (Umweltzonen) where vehicles must meet specific requirements, inner courtyards in Berlin Altbau where carry routes go through several gates, and apartment blocks in Hamburg where elevators are shared by many households and may only be used for moving at certain times of day.
about.desktop2: Our teams are used to combining Swedish planning culture with German precision. We coordinate departure from Sweden so arrival time matches the Hausmeister in Berlin, loading zones in Hamburg's canal-side districts or courtyard access hours in Munich where gates are locked in the evening. Our coordinator handles both Swedish and German communication and makes sure the driving plan, rests along the Autobahn and unloading times follow both local rules and your preferences.
about.desktop3: Every move to/from Germany includes complimentary box loans, free rescheduling up to 24 hours before move day and a 14-day guarantee on cleaning services. Support gives concrete advice on environmental zones in cities such as Berlin, Hamburg and Munich, how to plan around German driving/rest regulations for drivers, and what is good to know about shared stairwells, waste rooms and bicycle storage in German properties.
about.mobile: Specialized teams for moves to/from Germany who understand Autobahn, environmental zones and German properties.
about.mobileExpanded: We plan routes via the Autobahn, factor in environmental zones and ensure loading and unloading times match German regulations and building routines.
about.mobileExpanded2: One point of contact follows you from quote to move-in and helps with questions on German property rules, Hausmeister routines and how to navigate differences in housing structure.

experienceSection.title: Our experience with moves to and from Germany

experience.title: Our experience with moves to and from Germany
experience.description: We regularly move between Sweden and Germany – from apartments in Stockholm to Altbau buildings in Berlin, row houses in Hamburg suburbs and newly built apartments in Munich. We're used to planning around long driving distances, German driving/rest regulations, environmental zones where only certain vehicles may enter, and stairwells where both neighbours and Hausmeister have clear expectations on how a move should be carried out.
experience.expanded: For larger projects we review both the Swedish and German addresses in detail. In Berlin we check whether the property has an inner courtyard with height restrictions for trucks, whether the elevator can handle the furniture or if we need to build carry chains via the stairwell. In Hamburg we take into account narrow streets near canals, limited loading zones and residents who want clear time windows. In Munich we look at parking options outside new developments where many moves may happen simultaneously. Our experience means we can give realistic time estimates when Autobahn, environmental zones and German building routines all affect the move.

reviews.subtitle: Most recommended mover for moves to/from Germany
reviews.description: Clients who have moved to or from Germany often highlight how we managed the combination of long driving distances, German punctuality and local rules without losing control. Families moving to Berlin describe how we coordinated in good time with the Hausmeister, booked the elevator and aligned timings with both neighbours and the property owner. Customers moving to Hamburg and Munich particularly appreciate that we were clear about the driving plan via the Autobahn, how rest regulations affect arrival time, and that we still kept our promised time windows despite the distance.

awards.title: Flyttella awards for moves to and from Germany
awards.description: Our awards connected to Germany are often tied to moves where multiple moving parts must work together: departure from Sweden, driving through several countries, arrival in a German city with environmental zones, and move-in to a property where both timing and consideration for neighbours are critical. Partners highlight our ability to deliver to-the-minute at addresses in, for example, Berlin Mitte or older districts of Hamburg, even when we've passed roadworks, checks and rest regulations along the way. Completing these moves without damage to either belongings or stairwells is a key reason behind the recognition we've received on the Germany route.

servicesSection.title: Discover all our services for moves to/from Germany
servicesSection.description: Household moves between Sweden and Germany, packing, storage, cleaning, assembly and advisory services for cross-border moves. We handle driving plans via the Autobahn, factor in rest requirements, plan around environmental zones and coordinate logistics at both Swedish and German addresses.

blogSection.title: Guides and tips for moving to/from Germany
blogSection.description: Plan your move around distance and season, check whether your address is in an environmental zone, and clarify in advance how elevators, courtyards and Hausmeister routines work at your specific German property.

blog.title: Checklist: moving to/from Germany
blog.description: Review route and rest times, ensure vehicles meet environmental zone requirements, book any loading zones and check with the Hausmeister which times are approved for moves.

processSection.title: Our process
processSection.subtitle: How it works
processSection.description: Our moving process is simple, transparent and safe: fill out the form, get a quote within a minute, get personal follow-up the same day and sign digitally when everything feels right. The quote is based on your actual conditions – distance, route via the Autobahn, any environmental zones, living area, floors, elevator and parking distance – and can be supplemented with packing, cleaning or storage without hidden fees.

process.fillFormDesc: Describe addresses in Sweden and Germany, date, floors, elevator rules, parking, whether basement/attic should be emptied and if the address is in an environmental zone. Specify desired additional services and whether special coordination with the Hausmeister is needed.
process.quickQuoteDesc: You get a fixed quote with clear scope where route, rest times, any environmental zones and loading zones are included. If inspection of the German address is needed we book it before finalizing the price.
process.signConfirmDesc: After signing we prepare a detailed driving plan, book loading zones where possible and ensure loading and unloading times fit both Swedish and German conditions.
process.personalContactDesc: Your coordinator keeps you updated on timings, route choices, rest times and gives tips on how to prepare belongings for transport and move-in at German properties.
process.moveCompletedDesc: The team protects entrances, stairs and elevators, adjusts carry routes to German property structures and makes sure your belongings end up in the right place in your new home in Germany or Sweden.
process.satisfiedCustomerDesc: We do a joint walkthrough, adjust furniture placement and ensure any follow-up services such as cleaning or storage follow the plan.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions about moving to/from Germany

items.question: How do the Autobahn and rest regulations affect my move to Germany?
items.answer: We plan driving around both distance and German rest regulations for drivers. You get a clear schedule where we explain how breaks and any detours affect arrival time, so key handover and move-in can be planned without stress.

items.question: Do I need to think about environmental zones in German cities?
items.answer: Yes, many German cities have environmental zones where only vehicles with the right sticker may enter. We factor this into planning, ensure our vehicles meet the requirements and explain if your address requires special permits or arrangements.

items.question: How does move-in work in German apartment buildings?
items.answer: We're used to German properties where Hausmeister and neighbours have clear routines. We align times for elevator use, carry routes through courtyards and inform in good time so the move feels structured and respectful for everyone involved.

items.question: Can you help with packing for a long drive to Germany?
items.answer: Yes, our packing teams pack for longer distances, label boxes by room in a way that makes it easy to get everything in place even when the German address has a different layout, and protect fragile items extra carefully for roads, weather and multiple loadings.

items.question: How far in advance should I book a move to/from Germany?
items.answer: For moves to and from Germany we recommend 4–6 weeks in advance, especially if you're moving to a major city with environmental zones or during busy periods. We always try to solve urgent requests where the driving plan and regulations make it possible.
